question.content: Build the AC equivalent model stage by stage and derive Au, Ri, and Ro, then include the R4-short output conclusion. Output exactly one ASCII line with four semicolon-separated fields in the form Au:...;Ri:...;Ro:...;if_R4_short_uo:...; do not use '=' or any equation form, no Chinese characters, no prose sentence, and no extra text. Answer format: Au:...;Ri:...;Ro:...;if_R4_short_uo:... in that order; write only the final results, with no units, explanatory text, or equals signs.

step_1.content: Represent each amplifier block by small-signal two-port matrices and cascade from input to output.
step_1.key_point: Overall transfer and port resistances are read from composite matrix entries.

step_3.content: Form the terminated two-port of the follower-load block including R5 and RL.
step_3.key_point: Right block is reduced as a formal port transformation.

step_5.content: Cascade with the left transistor block to derive overall Au from the transfer matrix.
step_5.key_point: Au is obtained without ad-hoc staged voltage chaining.

step_7.content: Use driving-point formulas on the same composite matrix to get Ri and Ro; then set R4 short as a boundary-case matrix.
step_7.key_point: Shorted R4 boundary enforces zero output transfer.

step_9.content: Assemble final tokens in required key order Au;Ri;Ro;if_R4_short_uo.
step_9.key_point: Output line keeps exact dataset field format.
